actions.title: Largest-Ever National Health Care Fraud Takedown
actions.description: DOJ charged 324 defendants nationwide for alleged participation in health care fraud schemes targeting Medicare, Medicaid, and other federal programs. Schemes involved more than $14.6 billion in intended losses. CMS reportedly prevented more than $4 billion in fraudulent payments as a result of these efforts.

actions.title: Operation Gold Rush — $10.6 Billion Medicare Fraud Scheme
actions.description: Largest case amount ever charged by DOJ. Members of a transnational criminal organization orchestrated a Medicare fraud scheme totaling more than $10.6 billion. The criminals purchased dozens of medical supply companies in the U.S. and submitted more than $10 billion in fraudulent claims using the stolen identities of more than 1 million Americans.

actions.title: 74 Defendants Charged in Opioid Diversion Schemes
actions.description: As part of the National Health Care Fraud Takedown, DOJ charged 74 defendants across dozens of cases involving the illegal diversion of more than 15 million prescription opioid pills.

actions.title: Arizona: $1.1 Billion Medically Unnecessary Skin Graft Fraud
actions.description: Prosecutors charged 7 defendants, including 5 medical professionals, in connection with approximately $1.1 billion in fraudulent billing involving medically unnecessary skin grafts billed to Medicare and Medicaid.

actions.title: Kentucky Addiction Treatment Center Fraud — $8M+ in False Claims
actions.description: DOJ investigated and prosecuted an addiction treatment fraud scheme at Kentucky Addiction Centers, involving more than $8 million in false claims submitted to Medicare and Kentucky Medicaid. The centers operated clinics in Winchester, Paducah, Paintsville, and London, Kentucky. A federal jury later convicted Dr. José Alzadon, Michael Bregenzer, and Barbie Vanhoose in March 2025 for conspiracy to commit health care fraud, eight counts of health care fraud, and conspiracy to distribute controlled substances. See also the March 20, 2025 conviction entry for full case details.

actions.title: Florida ACA Enrollment Fraud — $233M in Fraudulent Claims
actions.description: DOJ resolved major cases involving fraudulent ACA enrollment schemes in Florida, in which brokers and marketing firms submitted false applications to obtain premium tax credits and subsidies. The cases involved more than $233 million in fraudulent claims, with at least $180 million paid out by the government.

actions.title: Brooklyn Defendants Plead Guilty: $68M Adult Day Care Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Multiple Brooklyn defendants pleaded guilty in connection with a $68 million adult day care Medicaid fraud scheme. The case involved adult day care centers in Brooklyn billing New York Medicaid for services never provided to beneficiaries. This case was later cited by CMS Administrator Dr. Oz as a prominent example when he launched a 50-question investigation into New York's $124 billion Medicaid program in March 2026, noting patterns of fraud in adult day care programming that significantly exceeded national norms. Prosecuted by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York.

actions.title: Florida Nursing Assistant Convicted: $11.4M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: A Florida nursing assistant was convicted for his or her role in an $11.4 million health care fraud scheme targeting Medicare beneficiaries. The conviction, secured by the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Southern District of Florida, reflects ongoing DOJ efforts to hold individual health care workers accountable for Medicare fraud, not just corporate entities.

actions.title: New DOJ Division for National Fraud Enforcement Created
actions.description: The administration announced the creation of a new DOJ Division for National Fraud Enforcement, designed to coordinate fraud enforcement across federal programs. The announcement explicitly referenced health care fraud enforcement activity in Minnesota. Health care fraud is expected to be a top priority.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Announces Plans to Audit Minnesota Medicaid; Defer High-Risk Payments
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Oz announced plans to audit Minnesota's Medicaid program and defer payments to high-risk programs, following the massive Minnesota Medicaid fraud scandal. CMS identified approximately 400 Medicaid-billed businesses operating out of a single building that generated approximately $380 million in taxpayer billing.

social_posts.post_text: Posted video documenting investigations in Minnesota, including roughly 400 Medicaid-billed businesses operating out of a single building generating approximately $380 million in taxpayer billing.

actions.title: Dr. Oz and Deputy Secretary O'Neill Fact-Finding Mission to Minnesota
actions.description: Dr. Oz and HHS Deputy Secretary Jim O'Neill conducted a fact-finding mission to Minnesota, signaling heightened federal oversight of the state's Medicaid program. O'Neill posted publicly about their findings, emphasizing the administration's focus on uncovering fraud.

social_posts.post_text: Committed to uncovering and stopping all fraud and waste states have allowed criminals to steal.

actions.title: Dr. Oz and Essayli Building Team to Investigate CA Hospice and Home-Care Fraud
actions.description: Dr. Oz (CMS) and First Assistant U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli announced they are working together to build a team investigating alleged widespread hospice and home-care fraud in Los Angeles County. CMS indicated this is a top priority.

social_posts.post_text: Posted multiple videos documenting on-the-ground investigations in LA County, stating CMS is prioritizing hospice and home-care fraud enforcement.
Working with Dr. Oz to investigate massive healthcare fraud occurring in California.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Calls on Governor Newsom to Produce Comprehensive CA Anti-Fraud Action Plan
actions.description: Dr. Oz publicly called on California Governor Gavin Newsom to produce a comprehensive action plan to address major fraud in the California health care system.

social_posts.post_text: Calling on Governor Newsom to produce a comprehensive action plan to address major fraud in the CA health care system.

actions.title: Dr. Oz: Hospice Fraud Spreading Beyond California; CMS Cracking Down
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz warned that hospice fraud—long concentrated in California—is expanding to other states, and that CMS is intensifying national enforcement. California has been the epicenter of sham hospice fraud, with multiple operators convicted (including the $16M sham hospice ring sentenced in November 2025 and Petros Fichidzhyan sentenced to 12 years). Dr. Oz indicated CMS is using data analytics to identify hospice providers with anomalous billing patterns, expanding scrutiny to Texas, Nevada, and other states where suspicious hospice enrollment has spiked. The CRUSH RFI published later in February 2026 specifically addresses hospice oversight.

social_posts.post_text: Hospice fraud is spreading beyond California, and CMS is cracking down.

actions.title: Trump Plans New Anti-Fraud Task Force for California, Led by JD Vance
actions.description: Reuters reported that President Trump plans to create a new anti-fraud task force focused on California, which will be led by Vice President JD Vance.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Maine Audit: $45.6M Improper Medicaid Payments for Autism Services
actions.description: HHS-OIG reported its Maine audit revealed Maine made at least $45.6 million in improper Medicaid payments for autism services for children. This is part of a series of audits looking into unallowable services and questionable billing patterns in Medicaid ABA services provided to children diagnosed with autism. To date, HHS-OIG has completed three state audits: Indiana, Wisconsin, and Maine.

social_posts.post_text: Committed to uncovering and stopping all fraud and waste states have allowed criminals to steal.

actions.title: CMS Closes Medicaid Financing Loophole Used by States to Shift Costs to Federal Government
actions.description: CMS announced action to close a Medicaid financing loophole used by states to shift Medicaid costs to the federal government, strengthening program integrity and reducing improper federal spending.

social_posts.post_text: With this rule, CMS is stopping these inappropriate financing schemes and ensuring every federal Medicaid dollar is used as Congress intended.

actions.title: CMS Withholds $300M from California Over Unlawful Health Care Spending on Illegal Immigrants
actions.description: Dr. Oz reported CMS will withhold nearly $300 million in Medicaid funding from California because of the scale of its unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants. CMS found more than $1.8 billion in unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants across 8 states.
actions.amount: $300M withheld from CA; $1.8B found across 8 states

social_posts.post_text: CMS will withhold nearly $300 million in Medicaid funding from California because of the scale of its unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants. CMS has found more than $1.8 billion in unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants across 8 states.

actions.title: California Must Return $1B+ After CMS Audit on Unlawful Immigrant Health Spending
actions.description: First Assistant U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli reported California must return more than $1 billion to the federal government after a CMS audit uncovered its massive unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants.

social_posts.post_text: California must return more than $1 billion to the federal government after a CMS audit uncovered its massive unlawful health care spending on illegal immigrants.

actions.title: HHS Secretary Kennedy: AI Being Used to Detect Medicare Fraud, Potential Billions in Savings
actions.description: HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. claimed the administration is using AI to detect fraud in Medicare, with potential savings of billions of dollars this year.

social_posts.post_text: @SecKennedy: "We are applying [AI] right now to Medicare — we are going to save billions of dollars this year. Medicaid and Medicare get $100 billion stolen from us every year and that's money that's not available for the poor and for seniors…[AI's] ability to detect fraud is extraordinary."

actions.title: DMERx CEO Gary Cox Convicted of $1B Medicare Fraud Conspiracy
actions.description: A federal jury in the Southern District of Florida convicted Gary Cox, 79, of Maricopa County, Arizona, CEO of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), of conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud and wire fraud, three counts of healthcare fraud, conspiracy to pay and receive healthcare kickbacks, and conspiracy to defraud the United States. Cox operated an internet-based platform generating false doctors' orders for orthotic braces and pain creams, targeting hundreds of thousands of Medicare beneficiaries through deceptive mailers, TV ads, and offshore call centers. Medicare paid more than $360 million of the $1 billion+ billed. Cox was sentenced December 19, 2025 to 15 years in prison and ordered to pay $452 million in restitution.

actions.title: Pakistan-Based Operator Indicted in $650M Arizona Medicaid Addiction Treatment Fraud
actions.description: Farrukh Jarar Ali, 41, of Pakistan, was indicted for conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud in connection with a $650 million scheme involving at least 41 sham substance abuse treatment clinics in Arizona. Ali's Pakistan-based company ProMD Solutions credentialed and enrolled these clinics with Arizona Medicaid (AHCCCS) from April 2021 to July 2023, but the clinics did not provide legitimate care. Patients—many recruited from homeless populations and Native American reservations—were offered kickbacks and free room and board. The clinics billed $650 million and received approximately $564 million in payments.

actions.title: DOJ and HHS Launch Joint False Claims Act Working Group with Six Healthcare Enforcement Priorities
actions.description: DOJ and HHS jointly announced the creation of the DOJ-HHS False Claims Act Working Group, co-led by DOJ's Civil Division and the HHS General Counsel, CMS Center for Program Integrity, and HHS-OIG counsel. The group established six priority enforcement areas: Medicare Advantage upcoding, drug/device pricing fraud, patient access barriers, kickback schemes, defective medical devices, and EHR manipulation driving unnecessary utilization. The working group coordinates referrals from HHS to DOJ, cross-agency investigations, and maximizes recoveries.

actions.title: Florida DME Owner Peter Roussonicolos Sentenced to 12 Years for $61.5M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Peter Roussonicolos, of Port Saint Lucie, Florida, was sentenced to 12 years in prison and 3 years supervised release for conspiring to defraud Medicare through five durable medical equipment supplier companies. Roussonicolos operated the companies as a silent partner while concealing his involvement due to prior felony convictions. The companies submitted approximately $61.5 million in false and fraudulent Medicare claims for medically unnecessary DME and were paid approximately $26.7 million. The scheme involved falsified enrollment forms and kickbacks to patient recruiters.

actions.title: DOJ Launches Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center Using AI and Cloud Analytics
actions.description: Announced as part of the 2025 National Healthcare Fraud Takedown, the DOJ launched the Health Care Fraud Data Fusion Center, bringing together experts from the DOJ Health Care Fraud Unit Data Analytics Team, FBI, HHS-OIG, CMS, DEA, and IRS-CI to detect emerging fraud schemes using cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and advanced analytics. The Center aims to break down inter-agency data silos and increase the efficiency of proactive fraud detection, implementing President Trump's executive order on eliminating federal data silos.

actions.title: CMS Finalizes Marketplace Integrity and Affordability Rule to Combat ACA Enrollment Fraud
actions.description: CMS finalized the Marketplace Integrity and Affordability Final Rule (effective August 25, 2025), targeting the surge of improper ACA exchange enrollments driven by unscrupulous agents and brokers. Key provisions include eliminating the 150% FPL Special Enrollment Period—a major driver of unauthorized enrollments—mandating pre-enrollment verification of SEP eligibility, and adopting a 'preponderance of evidence' standard for terminating agent/broker agreements. CMS estimated the rule will lower individual premiums by approximately 5% on average and save taxpayers up to $12 billion in 2026. CMS suspended 850 agents and brokers in 2024 and estimated approximately 200,000 consumers had plans changed without their consent.

actions.title: CMS and DOGE Find 2.8 Million Americans in Duplicate Medicaid/ACA Enrollments; $14B Annual Savings Identified
actions.description: CMS, in coordination with DOGE, announced the identification of 2.8 million Americans potentially enrolled in duplicate Medicaid/CHIP plans or simultaneously in both Medicaid/CHIP and subsidized ACA Exchange coverage: 1.2 million/month enrolled in Medicaid/CHIP across two or more states and 1.6 million/month enrolled in both Medicaid/CHIP and subsidized Exchange plans. CMS estimated $14 billion in annual taxpayer savings from eliminating duplicate enrollment. CMS directed states to recheck eligibility and provided 30-day notice to affected individuals. HHS Secretary RFK Jr. stated HHS staff 'uncovered millions who were illegally or improperly enrolled.'

actions.title: Troy Health Enters First-Ever Healthcare Non-Prosecution Agreement; AI Tool Used to Commit Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Troy Health, Inc. (d/b/a Troy Medicare), a North Carolina-based Medicare Advantage plan, entered into an 18-month Non-Prosecution Agreement (NPA) and agreed to pay $1.43 million—the first NPA under DOJ's updated Corporate Enforcement Policy and the first healthcare resolution involving misconduct facilitated by artificial intelligence. Troy misused its proprietary AI platform 'Troy.ai' to offer kickbacks to pharmacies for Medicare beneficiary data and to batch-enroll beneficiaries without consent. At the scheme's height, Troy enrolled over 300 beneficiaries in a single day approximately one minute apart.
officials: Matthew R. Galeotti (Acting AAG, Criminal Division)

actions.title: DOJ Expands Health Care Fraud Strike Force to Massachusetts (Boston)
actions.description: The DOJ's Criminal Division announced the expansion of the Health Care Fraud Unit's New England Strike Force into the District of Massachusetts, embedding federal law enforcement—FBI, HHS-OIG, FDA, DEA, HSI, VA-OIG, and IRS-CI—alongside the existing U.S. Attorney's Office Health Care Fraud Unit in Boston. The expansion was driven by Massachusetts and Boston's status as a national hub for healthcare and life sciences, with particular focus on pharmaceutical/medical device company fraud, provider misconduct, and complex schemes targeting Medicare/Medicaid. In FY2025 alone, Massachusetts recovered over $450 million in coordinated actions.
officials: Matthew R. Galeotti (Acting AAG, Criminal Division)
Leah B. Foley (U.S. Attorney, D. Mass.)

actions.title: True Health Diagnostics CEO, Physicians, and Marketers Pay $6M+ to Settle Lab Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Christopher Grottenthaler, former CEO of True Health Diagnostics, Dr. Hong Davis, Dr. Elizabeth Seymour, and seven marketers agreed to pay a combined $6,068,462 to resolve False Claims Act allegations of a healthcare kickback scheme. Defendants allegedly offered and paid doctors kickbacks disguised as MSO (Management Service Organization) distributions and consulting fees to induce laboratory testing referrals, submitting false claims to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE from January 2015 to January 2018. The Eastern District of Texas prosecuted the case.

actions.title: Queens, NY Doctor Alexander Baldonado Sentenced to 7 Years for $24M Medicare Fraud Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Dr. Alexander Baldonado, M.D., 69, of Queens, New York, was sentenced to seven years in prison and ordered to pay $2.21 million in restitution following his conviction at a five-day jury trial in February 2025. Baldonado received illegal cash kickbacks and bribes in exchange for ordering medically unnecessary laboratory tests (including cancer genetic tests) and orthotic braces, submitting over $24 million in fraudulent Medicare claims. Medicare actually paid approximately $2.2 million on these claims.

actions.title: Four California Sham Hospice Operators Sentenced in $16M Medicare Fraud and Money Laundering Scheme
actions.description: Four California defendants were sentenced for their roles in operating sham hospice companies that billed Medicare nearly $16 million for medically unnecessary and never-provided hospice services from July 2019 to January 2023. Sentences: Juan Carlos Esparza (57 months), Karpis Srapyan (57 months), Mihran Panosyan (57 months), and Petros Fichidzhyan (12 years—sentenced separately in April 2025). The scheme used foreign nationals' identities as purported hospice owners and laundered proceeds through shell companies and bank accounts.

actions.title: Done Telehealth CEO and Clinical President Convicted in $100M Adderall Distribution and Healthcare Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury in San Francisco convicted Ruthia He, founder and CEO of Done (California digital health company), and David Brody, its clinical president, for illegally distributing over 40 million Adderall pills online and conspiring to commit healthcare fraud. The defendants spent $40 million on deceptive social media advertising to attract drug seekers, defrauded insurers of approximately $14 million through false prior authorization requests, and He relocated Done to China to obstruct the DOJ investigation and destroy incriminating documents. He was also convicted of conspiracy to obstruct justice. Sentencing was scheduled for February 25, 2026.

actions.title: Atrium Health and Senior Living (KBWB) and CEO Sentenced for $146M Healthcare Fraud and Tax Conspiracy
actions.description: KBWB Operations LLC (d/b/a Atrium Health and Senior Living) and its former CEO/managing member Kevin Breslin, 58, of Hoboken, NJ, were sentenced following December 2024 guilty pleas. Breslin received 90 months in prison; both Breslin and KBWB were ordered to pay $146 million in restitution and $8.4 million in forfeiture. The defendants diverted CMS funds intended for resident care at 23 skilled nursing facilities in New Jersey, Wisconsin, and Michigan—prioritizing owner distributions, diverting employee health insurance premiums and 401(k) contributions, and failing to remit employment taxes to the IRS.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: CMS Put $11.2 Billion at Risk by Not Properly Closing Federal Contracts
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-04-24-02048 found that CMS failed to meet administrative closeout requirements for 50 contracts totaling $11.2 billion reviewed for the period October 2018–September 2023. Twelve contracts worth $2.1 billion remained overdue for closeout at audit conclusion, putting those funds at risk of fraud, waste, and abuse. OIG recommended CMS complete administrative closeout for the 12 delinquent contracts and develop clear policies defining documentation requirements and roles for timely closure. CMS concurred with both recommendations.

actions.title: Co-Conspirator Pleads Guilty in $54M California Radiology and Hospice Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Sophia Shaklian, 38, of Los Angeles, pleaded guilty to her role in a $54 million Medicare fraud scheme involving seven healthcare providers—including radiology imaging centers and a hospice—across Los Angeles County from 2019–2024, billing for services never performed. Shaklian managed and submitted false claims for the providers. Co-conspirator Alex Alexsanian, 48, of Burbank, pleaded guilty to money laundering conspiracy on January 20, 2026, and agreed to forfeit $3 million. The pair pocketed $23 million in total, laundering proceeds through a fake identity and using funds to buy $6 million in gold bars.

actions.title: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute Pays $15M to Settle NIH Research Grant Fraud Allegations
actions.description: Dana-Farber Cancer Institute agreed to pay $15 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that from 2014–2024 it made materially false statements and certifications related to NIH research grants. Specifically, researchers authored papers containing duplicated and manipulated images/data from 14 scientific publications, with six NIH grants funding the fraudulent research and four additional NIH grants obtained through false applications. Welsh microbiologist and whistleblower Sholto David received $2.625 million under qui tam provisions. U.S. Attorney Leah B. Foley noted 'there is no place in scientific research for fraud, waste and abuse.'
officials: Leah B. Foley (U.S. Attorney, D. Mass.)

actions.title: HHS-OIG Medicare Advantage Audit of Humana Louisiana Finds $6.8M in Unsupported Diagnosis Overpayments
actions.description: HHS-OIG published a compliance audit of Humana Health Benefit of Louisiana (Contract H1951) for Medicare Advantage risk adjustment payments for 2017–2018. The audit found that most selected diagnosis codes did not comply with Federal requirements, estimating Humana was overpaid approximately $10 million for the audited contract when extrapolated, with OIG recommending Humana refund $6,777,385 to the Federal Government. Most unsupported diagnoses resulted from past medical conditions improperly reported as current conditions, or from radiology/ECG reports not interpreted and signed by an acceptable provider type.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Illinois Made $9.5M in Unallowable Medicaid Capitation Payments for Incarcerated Enrollees
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-05-24-00019 found Illinois made unallowable capitation payments to managed care organizations for incarcerated Medicaid enrollees who were ineligible for coverage. In a sample of 100 incarcerated enrollees, 48 had unallowable payments totaling $263,186. Extrapolated to the audit period, OIG estimated at least $9.5 million ($8.3 million federal share) in unallowable payments. OIG recommended Illinois refund $8,366,521 in federal funds and expand its automated enrollment termination process to cover inmates outside Illinois DOC facilities. Illinois accepted both recommendations.

actions.title: DMERx CEO Gary Cox Sentenced to 15 Years in Prison; $452M Restitution for $1B Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Gary Cox, 79, of Maricopa County, Arizona, CEO of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), was sentenced to 15 years in prison and ordered to pay over $452 million in restitution following his June 2025 conviction for conspiring to defraud Medicare of more than $1 billion. The FBI, HHS-OIG, VA-OIG, and DCIS investigated the case. This represents one of the longest individual sentences for Medicare fraud in recent years.

actions.title: HHS-OIG: Medicaid Agencies Made $207.5M in Unallowable Capitation Payments for Deceased Enrollees
actions.description: HHS-OIG Report A-04-23-09010 estimated that Medicaid agencies made $207.5 million ($138.6 million federal share) in unallowable capitation payments to MCOs for enrollees whose deaths preceded the monthly service periods during FY2022 (July 2021–June 2022). In a sample of 100 payments, 99 were unallowable—49 already not recovered or recovered only after OIG notification. This represents OIG's 18th such audit since 2016, which cumulatively identified approximately $289 million in improper capitation payments for deceased enrollees. OIG recommended CMS provide matched T-MSIS data to states for recovery.

actions.title: Georgia Man Patrick Moore Sentenced to 46 Months for $24M Medicare Genetic Testing Kickback Scheme
actions.description: Patrick C. Moore Jr., of Peachtree City, Georgia, was sentenced to 46 months in prison and ordered to pay $7.2 million in restitution for his role orchestrating a network of recruiters targeting Medicare beneficiaries for medically unnecessary genetic tests. Moore billed approximately $24 million to Medicare and received approximately $4.3 million in illegal kickbacks. He pleaded guilty in May 2025 to one count of conspiracy to defraud the United States and to pay and receive illegal healthcare kickbacks.

actions.title: Virginia Laboratory NEXT Molecular Analytics Pays $758K+ to Settle Medicare Kickback Allegations
actions.description: NEXT Bio-Research Services LLC (d/b/a NEXT Molecular Analytics), a Chester, Virginia clinical laboratory, agreed to pay at least $758,000 plus contingent amounts to resolve False Claims Act allegations of paying illegal kickbacks to physicians and independent contractor marketers disguised as consulting fees and medical director fees—paid on a per-referral basis—to induce laboratory testing referrals. The kickback scheme involved doctors in Texas and Arkansas.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Spring 2025 Semiannual Report: $16.6B in Healthcare Fraud Flagged (Oct 2024–Mar 2025)
actions.description: HHS-OIG's Spring 2025 Semiannual Report to Congress—the first under the current administration—identified more than $16.6 billion in healthcare fraud, overpayments, and improper payments for the period October 1, 2024 through March 31, 2025. HHS-OIG conducted 946 criminal, civil, and administrative investigations, completed 744 enforcement actions, excluded 1,503 individuals and entities from federal healthcare programs, made 1,209 criminal referrals, and pursued 395 civil actions. Key focus areas included home health fraud, drug diversion, COVID-19 fraud, and Medicare patient abuse and neglect.

actions.title: HHS-OIG Fall 2025 Semiannual Report: $19.04B in Monetary Impact (Apr–Sep 2025)
actions.description: HHS-OIG's Fall 2025 Semiannual Report to Congress identified $19.04 billion in monetary impact for the period April 1 through September 30, 2025—reflecting a significant increase from the Spring 2025 report's $16.6 billion. The period covered the 2025 National Health Care Fraud Takedown (June 30, 2025) and related enforcement actions. The report covers the peak enforcement period including the $14.6 billion takedown, the $10.6 billion Operation Gold Rush EDNY indictment, and the launch of the Healthcare Fraud Data Fusion Center.

actions.title: DOJ Announces Record $6.8B in False Claims Act Recoveries in FY2025; Healthcare Accounts for $5.7B
actions.description: DOJ announced that False Claims Act settlements and judgments exceeded $6.8 billion in FY2025 (ending September 30, 2025)—the highest single-year total in FCA history. Over $5.7 billion of that total involved the healthcare industry, restoring funds to Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE. Whistleblowers filed 1,297 qui tam lawsuits in FY2025 (a new record, surpassing 2024's 980), recovering over $5.3 billion. Since 1986, cumulative FCA recoveries have exceeded $85 billion.

actions.title: CMS FY2025 Improper Payments Fact Sheet: $28.8B Medicare FFS, $23.7B Medicare Advantage
actions.description: CMS published its FY2025 Improper Payments Fact Sheet estimating Medicare Fee-for-Service improper payments at $28.83 billion (6.55% rate), down from $31.70 billion in FY2024. Medicare Advantage (Part C) improper payments rose to $23.67 billion (6.09% rate), up from $19.07 billion, primarily driven by unsupported diagnoses submitted by MA organizations. Medicaid improper payments were $37.39 billion (6.12% rate). CHIP improper payments were $1.37 billion (7.05% rate). CMS noted that most improper payments reflect documentation issues rather than fraud per se.

actions.title: White House Announces New DOJ National Fraud Enforcement Division; Healthcare Fraud a Core Focus
actions.description: The Trump White House announced the creation of a new Department of Justice Division for National Fraud Enforcement, to be led by an Assistant Attorney General appointed by the President. The division will enforce federal criminal and civil laws against fraud targeting federal government programs—including Medicare, Medicaid, and other HHS-funded programs—federally funded benefits, businesses, nonprofits, and private citizens. VP J.D. Vance stated the division 'will be run out of the White House.' On January 28, 2026, President Trump announced Colin McDonald as the nominee for AAG of the new division.

actions.title: Kaiser Permanente Affiliates Pay $556M to Resolve Medicare Advantage False Claims Act Allegations
actions.description: Kaiser Foundation Health Plan Inc. and affiliated Kaiser entities in California and Colorado agreed to pay $556 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that Kaiser systematically pressured physicians to alter patient medical records after visits by adding diagnoses not considered or addressed during those visits—mining patients' histories and sending physician 'queries' months or over a year after visits to generate addenda for unsupported diagnoses, and linking physician bonuses to meeting diagnosis goals. This is one of the largest Medicare Advantage risk-adjustment fraud settlements in history.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: Five Florida Ophthalmology Practices Pay $6M to Resolve Fraudulent TCD Ultrasound Kickback Claims
actions.description: Five Florida ophthalmology practices—Clay Eye Holdings LLC, Retina Macula Specialist of Miami LLC, Florida Eye Institute P.A., Miami Eye LLC, and Kendall Eye Institute Inc.—agreed to pay nearly $6 million total to resolve False Claims Act allegations of billing Medicare and Medicaid for medically unnecessary trans-cranial Doppler ultrasound (TCD) tests through a kickback arrangement with a third-party testing company. Practices performed TCDs on thousands of patients and pre-labeled them with serious diagnoses (that nearly all patients did not have) before tests were complete. The State of Florida received $333,500 for its Medicaid share; the whistleblower received $1.135 million.
officials: Gregory W. Kehoe (U.S. Attorney, M.D. Fla.)

actions.title: Massachusetts Sleep Doctor Dr. Pankaj Merchia Convicted of Healthcare Fraud, Money Laundering, and Tax Evasion
actions.description: A federal jury in Boston convicted Dr. Pankaj Merchia, a Harvard-educated sleep medicine physician, for healthcare fraud, three counts of money laundering, conspiracy to defraud the IRS, and two counts of tax evasion. Merchia fraudulently billed insurance companies millions of dollars for sleep apnea machines not used by patients for years (2017–2019), billed over $390,000 for fraudulent family member treatment claims, and failed to report over $6.5 million in income (2009–2019) from his medical businesses. He purchased a $2.1 million Massachusetts home with fraud proceeds. Sentencing scheduled for April 28, 2026.

actions.title: CMS Issues Final Rule Closing Medicaid Provider Tax Loophole; $78.2B Decade Savings
actions.description: CMS finalized the 'Preserving Medicaid Funding for Vulnerable Populations—Closing a Health Care-Related Tax Loophole Final Rule,' codifying the Medicaid provider tax provisions adopted by Congress in the Working Families Tax Cuts legislation (Public Law 119-21, signed July 4, 2025). The rule prohibits states from taxing Medicaid business at higher rates than non-Medicaid, blocks indirect tax structures disguising disproportionate Medicaid burdens, and strengthens CMS enforcement authority. CMS estimates the rule saves the federal government $78.2 billion over the next decade, addressing financing gimmicks that drew down over $24 billion annually. Effective April 3, 2026, with phased state compliance deadlines through FY2028.

actions.title: Two Queens, NY Men Charged with $120M Adult Day Care and Pharmacy Medicare/Medicaid Fraud
actions.description: Inwoo Kim (aka 'Tony Kim'), 42, and Daniel Lee (aka 'Daniel Yang'), 56, both of Flushing, Queens, were charged with conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud. Kim owned a pharmacy and two social adult day care centers—Royal Adult Daycare and Happy Life Inc.—where from 2016–2026 the defendants paid illegal cash bribes and supermarket gift certificates to beneficiaries to fill prescriptions and enroll in day care services, and submitted claims for services never provided. In total, Medicare and Medicaid paid approximately $120 million for medically unnecessary or non-provided prescription drugs and adult day care services.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: Two Executives Sentenced to 20 Years Each for $233M ACA Enrollment Fraud Targeting Homeless and Vulnerable
actions.description: Cory Lloyd, 47, of Stuart, Florida, and Steven Strong, 43, of Mansfield, Texas, were each sentenced to 20 years in federal prison and ordered to pay $180.6 million in restitution following their November 2025 jury convictions for a multi-year scheme defrauding the Affordable Care Act subsidy program of approximately $233 million. The defendants used 'street marketers' to fraudulently enroll homeless individuals, people experiencing mental health crises, and substance abuse patients into fully subsidized ACA plans to collect insurance commission payments. A third defendant, Dafud Iza, received 35 months. AG Pam Bondi called the scheme 'evil and unforgivable.'

actions.title: Former NFL Player Keith Gray Convicted in $328M Medicare Genetic Testing Fraud Scheme
actions.description: A federal jury in Dallas convicted Keith J. Gray, 39, of McKinney, Texas—a former NFL/Carolina Panthers player—and owner of Axis Professional Labs LLC and Kingdom Health Laboratory LLC, for conspiracy to defraud the United States, conspiracy to pay/receive healthcare kickbacks (five AKS counts), and three counts of money laundering. Gray billed Medicare approximately $328 million (Medicare paid ~$54 million) for medically unnecessary cardiovascular genetic tests by paying kickbacks to marketers for patient DNA samples, Medicare numbers, and signed physician orders. Gray laundered proceeds through luxury vehicle purchases.

actions.title: Texas Orthopedic Surgeon Dr. Michael Taba Sentenced to 8.5 Years for $145M DOL Workers' Comp Prescription Fraud
actions.description: Dr. Michael Taba, of McKinney, Texas, was sentenced to 102 months (8.5 years) in prison and ordered to pay over $13 million in restitution following his November 2023 conviction for conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud and three counts of healthcare fraud. Taba accepted bribes from pharmacy owners to prescribe medically unnecessary compound creams to injured federal workers. From May 2014 to March 2017, pharmacies billed the Department of Labor's Office of Workers' Compensation Programs and Blue Cross Blue Shield more than $145 million and received more than $90 million for Taba-referred prescriptions.

actions.title: Arizona OASIS Hospital/USPI Pay $5.6M to Resolve Improper Financial Arrangement with Physicians
actions.description: Southwest Orthopedic and Spine Hospital LLC (d/b/a OASIS Hospital), United Surgical Partners International Inc. (USPI), and an affiliated entity agreed to pay $5.6 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that OASIS made improper interest payments on convertible bonds to a physician group that referred patients to OASIS, violating both the Anti-Kickback Statute and the Stark Law (Physician Self-Referral Law) from 2011–2018.
officials: Scott J. Lampert (HHS-OIG Acting Deputy IG)

actions.title: CMS/HHS Announce Major Anti-Fraud Package: $259.5M MN Deferral, DMEPOS Moratorium, CRUSH Initiative
actions.description: Vice President J.D. Vance, HHS Secretary RFK Jr., and CMS Administrator Dr. Oz jointly announced a sweeping anti-fraud package at the White House. Actions included: (1) $259.5 million deferral of quarterly federal Medicaid matching funds to Minnesota ($243.8M for fraudulent claims; $15.4M for unauthorized immigrant claims); (2) a nationwide six-month moratorium on new Medicare DMEPOS supplier enrollment, building on $1.5 billion in prevented DMEPOS billing in 2025; (3) the CRUSH (Comprehensive Regulations to Uncover Suspicious Healthcare) initiative RFI published in the Federal Register; (4) CMS-State Tax Fraud Partnership with 28 states. CMS metrics for 2025: $5.7 billion suspended, 122,658 claims denied, 5,586 providers revoked, 372 fraud referrals totaling $3.7 billion.

actions.title: Atlanta Gastroenterology Associates Pays $4.75M to Settle Kickbacks and Unnecessary Testing Allegations
actions.description: Atlanta Gastroenterology Associates (Georgia) and Advanced Pathology Solutions (Arkansas) agreed to pay $4.75 million combined to resolve False Claims Act allegations that Atlanta Gastroenterology received kickbacks in exchange for patient referrals to Advanced Pathology Solutions, and that APS performed medically unnecessary gastrointestinal pathology testing on those patients through automatic 'reflex ordering' without medical justification.
officials: Jonathan D. Ross (U.S. Attorney, E.D. Ark.)

actions.title: CMS Publishes CRUSH RFI in Federal Register; Seeks Public Input on Sweeping Anti-Fraud Regulation
actions.description: CMS published a Request for Information (RFI) in the Federal Register as part of its Comprehensive Regulations to Uncover Suspicious Healthcare (CRUSH) initiative, signaling a forthcoming major anti-fraud rulemaking. The RFI covers over a dozen subject areas including enhanced provider enrollment screening, identity proofing, preclusion list reforms, laboratory test fraud, DMEPOS supplier oversight, claim filing deadlines, AI-assisted coding oversight, beneficiary solicitation protections, surety bonds, Medicaid and CHIP fraud, and Marketplace program integrity. Public comments due March 30, 2026.

actions.title: Dr. Oz Launches Medicaid Fraud Investigation of New York; Sends 50-Question Letter to Gov. Hochul
actions.description: CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz sent a letter to New York Gov. Kathy Hochul posing 50 detailed questions about fraud, waste, and abuse in New York's $124 billion Medicaid program—the largest state Medicaid program in the country. Oz cited serious concerns about New York's oversight of personal care, home health, adult day care programming, non-emergency medical transportation, and behavioral health services, noting per-capita spending and workforce utilization patterns significantly exceeding national norms. Hochul was given 30 days to respond with a corrective action plan or face potential deferral of federal Medicaid payments. CMS cited the $68 million Brooklyn adult day care fraud as an example.

actions.title: Independent Health Agrees to Pay Up to $98M to Settle Medicare Advantage Upcoding Allegations
actions.description: Independent Health Association and Independent Health Corporation (Buffalo, NY) agreed to pay up to $98 million—$34.5 million guaranteed plus up to $63.5 million contingent—to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they submitted or caused the submission of invalid diagnosis codes to Medicare for Medicare Advantage enrollees to increase risk-adjusted payments. Co-defendant DxID LLC (a risk adjustment vendor) and its CEO Betsy Gaffney separately agreed to pay $2 million. Independent Health entered a five-year corporate integrity agreement with HHS-OIG requiring independent review of MA patients' medical records annually.

actions.title: C.R. Bard & Affiliates Pay $17M for Catheter Kickback Scheme
actions.description: C.R. Bard, Inc. and affiliates Liberator Medical Supply, Liberator Holdings, and Rochester Medical Corporation agreed to pay $17 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they provided free samples and discounts to urology practice groups as illegal kickbacks in exchange for using Bard's proprietary 'Link' prescription form for intermittent catheters. The scheme ran from 2016 through February 2020. A former Bard employee filed the qui tam whistleblower lawsuit.

actions.title: Pfizer (Biohaven) Pays $59.7M for Physician Kickbacks via Speaker Programs on Nurtec ODT
actions.description: Pfizer Inc., on behalf of subsidiary Biohaven Pharmaceutical, agreed to pay $59,746,277 to resolve False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback Statute allegations. From March 2020 through September 2022, Biohaven paid physician speaker honoraria and provided lavish meals at high-end restaurants to induce prescriptions of the migraine drug Nurtec ODT. Programs included attendees with no legitimate educational need such as family members, and some prescribers attended duplicate sessions with no educational value.

actions.title: Jeffrey King (Apex Medical) Pleads Guilty to $1.2B Wound Graft Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Jeffrey King, 46, of Phoenix, Arizona, co-owner of Apex Medical LLC and Viking Medical Consultants LLC, pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud for his role in a $1.2 billion scheme involving medically unnecessary amniotic wound grafts applied to elderly and hospice patients. King and co-conspirators instructed untrained sales reps to order grafts in maximum sizes regardless of wound size to maximize reimbursement. They submitted $1,212,005,778 in false claims — over $960 million targeting Medicare, TRICARE, and CHAMPVA.

actions.title: California Man Pleads Guilty to $17M Sham Hospice and Home Health Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Petros Fichidzhyan, 43, of Granada Hills, California, pleaded guilty to health care fraud, aggravated identity theft, and money laundering for orchestrating a years-long scheme to defraud Medicare of more than $17 million through sham hospice companies and a home health care agency. Fichidzhyan and co-schemers used stolen identities of foreign nationals as purported owners of the hospices and submitted false claims for services not provided. He also wrongfully obtained over $1 million for a home health agency by fraudulently using a doctor's identity.

actions.title: Medisca Inc. Pays $21.75M for Inflated AWPs on Compounded Prescription Ingredients
actions.description: Compound ingredient supplier Medisca Inc. agreed to pay $21.75 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly inflated Average Wholesale Prices (AWPs) for two compound prescription ingredients — resveratrol and mometasone furoate — to increase reimbursements its pharmacy customers received from federal health care programs including TRICARE and DOL Workers' Compensation. Medisca bought resveratrol for ~$0.37/gram and reported an AWP of $777/gram. A compounding pharmacist whistleblower will receive $3.4 million.

actions.title: Saint Vincent's Catholic Medical Centers (NY) Pays $29M for Retaining Military Healthcare Overpayments
actions.description: SVCMC Inc. (formerly Saint Vincents Catholic Medical Centers of New York) agreed to pay $29 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that it knowingly retained erroneously inflated payments from the Department of Defense for healthcare services provided to retired military members and their families. Saint Vincent's allegedly learned of calculation errors resulting in overpayments in 2012 but failed to notify the government or repay the funds, continuing to submit invoices at the inflated rates.

actions.title: Health Net Federal Services & Centene Pay $11.25M for Cybersecurity False Claims (TRICARE)
actions.description: Health Net Federal Services LLC and its parent Centene Corporation agreed to pay $11,253,400 to resolve False Claims Act allegations stemming from cybersecurity failures. From 2015 through 2018, HNFS allegedly failed to meet cybersecurity controls and falsely certified compliance in annual reports to the Defense Health Agency under its TRICARE contract, including failing to timely scan for known vulnerabilities and remediate security flaws on its networks.

actions.title: DMERx VP Gregory Schreck Pleads Guilty to $1B Medicare Fraud Conspiracy
actions.description: Gregory Schreck, 50, Vice President of Power Mobility Doctor Rx LLC (DMERx), pleaded guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud. Schreck admitted he and co-conspirators used the DMERx internet platform to generate false doctors' orders, connecting DME suppliers and pharmacies with telemedicine companies that accepted illegal kickbacks in exchange for signed orders. The DME suppliers and pharmacies paid kickbacks and billed Medicare and other insurers more than $1 billion, of which Medicare paid more than $360 million.

actions.title: Three Convicted for $8M Medicare/Medicaid Fraud Through Kentucky Opioid Addiction Clinics
actions.description: A federal jury convicted Dr. José Alzadon, Michael Bregenzer, and Barbie Vanhoose for their roles in a scheme to fraudulently bill Medicare and Kentucky Medicaid for over $8 million through Kentucky Addiction Centers, operating clinics in Winchester, Paducah, Paintsville, and London. Each defendant was convicted of conspiracy to commit health care fraud, eight counts of health care fraud, and conspiracy to distribute controlled substances.

actions.title: Seoul Medical Group Pays $62.85M for Medicare Advantage Diagnosis Upcoding
actions.description: Seoul Medical Group, its subsidiary Advanced Medical Management, the former president, and an affiliated radiology group collectively agreed to pay over $62.85 million to resolve False Claims Act allegations that they fraudulently increased Medicare Advantage reimbursements by submitting unsupported diagnosis codes for severe spinal conditions for patients who did not have those conditions.

actions.title: Diopsys Inc. Pays Up to $14.25M for Off-Label Vision Device Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Diopsys Inc. agreed to pay up to $14.25 million to resolve False Claims Act and state law allegations that the company caused healthcare providers to submit false claims to Medicare and Medicaid for its NOVA device used for electroretinography testing — an indication for which the device lacked FDA clearance — between January 2015 and December 2021. The case arose from a qui tam suit by a California ophthalmologist.

actions.title: DOJ Files FCA Complaint Against Vohra Wound Physicians for Medicare Overbilling in Nursing Homes
actions.description: The Department of Justice filed a False Claims Act complaint against Vohra Wound Physicians Management LLC, its founder Dr. Ameet Vohra, and VHS Holdings PA. The complaint alleges Vohra engaged in a nationwide scheme to bill Medicare for medically unnecessary and upcoded wound care services in skilled nursing facilities, using proprietary software that automatically billed higher-reimbursed surgical debridements and corporate revenue quotas that pressured physicians to perform unnecessary procedures.

actions.title: Walgreens Agrees to Pay Up to $350M for Illegally Filling Opioid Prescriptions and False Medicare Claims
actions.description: The Department of Justice, DEA, and HHS-OIG announced that Walgreens Boots Alliance agreed to pay up to $350 million to resolve allegations that Walgreens knowingly filled millions of unlawful controlled substance prescriptions from August 2012 through March 2023 and submitted false claims to federal health care programs. The government alleged Walgreens pressured pharmacists to fill prescriptions quickly without proper verification. The settlement includes a seven-year DEA compliance agreement and a five-year HHS-OIG Corporate Integrity Agreement.

actions.title: Gilead Sciences Pays $202M for Illegal Speaker Program Kickbacks on HIV Drugs
actions.description: The U.S. Attorney for the Southern District of New York announced a $202 million settlement with Gilead Sciences to resolve False Claims Act and Anti-Kickback Statute allegations. Between 2011 and 2017, Gilead paid 548 healthcare providers more than $23.7 million in honoraria, meals, and travel through sham speaker programs to induce prescriptions of HIV medications. The settlement provides $49 million for Medicaid programs nationwide, with the remainder going to Medicare, TRICARE, and the AIDS Drug Assistance Program.

actions.title: DOJ Files FCA Complaint Against Aetna, Humana, Elevance & Brokers for $100Ms in Medicare Advantage Kickbacks
actions.description: The Department of Justice filed a False Claims Act complaint against three of the nation's largest Medicare Advantage insurers — Aetna, Elevance Health (formerly Anthem), and Humana — and three major brokers — eHealth, GoHealth, and SelectQuote — alleging they paid and received hundreds of millions of dollars in illegal kickbacks for Medicare Advantage enrollment steering from 2016 through at least 2021. Aetna and Humana also allegedly conspired to discriminate against Medicare beneficiaries with disabilities, pressuring brokers to enroll fewer disabled beneficiaries perceived as less profitable.

actions.title: California Hospice Fraudster Petros Fichidzhyan Sentenced to 12 Years for $17M Medicare Fraud
actions.description: Petros Fichidzhyan, 44, of Granada Hills, California was sentenced to 12 years in prison and ordered to pay $17,129,060 in restitution for operating sham hospice companies and a fraudulent home health care agency that defrauded Medicare of more than $17 million. Fichidzhyan and co-conspirators used stolen identities of foreign nationals as purported hospice owners and billed for services not provided. Two homes purchased with fraud proceeds were forfeited.

actions.title: DOJ Criminal Division Names Health Care Fraud Top Enforcement Priority in New White-Collar Plan
actions.description: DOJ's Criminal Division head Matthew Galeotti announced the department's first-ever White-Collar Enforcement Plan, explicitly naming 'waste, fraud, and abuse, including healthcare fraud and federal program fraud' as the number one enforcement priority. The plan also updated the Corporate Enforcement Policy, revised monitor selection guidance, and expanded the DOJ Whistleblower Pilot Program to cover tips about health care fraud involving public health care programs and fraud against patients and private insurers.

actions.title: Fresno Community Hospital Pays $31.5M for Anti-Kickback and Stark Law Violations
actions.description: Fresno Community Hospital and Medical Center and its technology affiliate Physicians Network Advantage Inc. agreed to pay $31.5 million and enter a five-year Corporate Integrity Agreement to resolve False Claims Act, Anti-Kickback Statute, and Stark Law allegations. The hospital allegedly provided improper inducements to referring physicians including meals, alcohol, cigars, and substantial EHR software subsidies beginning in 2013. A whistleblower will receive approximately $5 million.

actions.title: Cuban National Julian Lopez Sentenced for $3.2M Miami Medicare DME Fraud
actions.description: Julian Lopez, 55, a Cuban citizen residing in Miami-Dade County, Florida, was sentenced to 30 months in prison and ordered to pay $1.5 million in restitution for his role in a $3.2 million Medicare fraud scheme. Lopez obtained Medicare beneficiary identification cards and sold the personal information to a durable medical equipment company, which billed Medicare for orthotic braces never provided to beneficiaries.

actions.title: Massachusetts DME Owner Raju Sharma Pleads Guilty to $30M Medicare Telemedicine Fraud
actions.description: Raju Sharma, 61, of Sharon, Massachusetts, owner of Pharmagears LLC and RR Medco LLC, agreed to plead guilty to conspiracy to commit health care fraud for his role in a nearly $30 million fraud scheme. Between February 2021 and February 2025, Sharma contracted with telemarketing companies to contact Medicare beneficiaries and generate orders for medically unnecessary orthotics via brief telemedicine encounters. His companies billed Medicare approximately $29.6 million and were paid approximately $15.8 million. Sharma used proceeds to purchase three Rolex watches, two Ferraris, and a Mercedes-Benz.

actions.title: CMS Expands Medicare Advantage RADV Audits to All ~550 Plans; Grows Auditor Workforce 50x
actions.description: CMS announced a major expansion of Risk Adjustment Data Validation (RADV) audits targeting Medicare Advantage plans, expanding from approximately 60 plans to all eligible MA contracts (approximately 550 plans). Records audited per plan will increase from 35 to between 35 and 200. CMS will grow its medical coding workforce from 40 to approximately 2,000 by September 1, 2025, deploy advanced record-review technology, and complete all outstanding audits for payment years 2018–2024 by early 2026. Federal estimates suggest MA plans may overbill by $17–43 billion annually.

actions.title: Chesapeake Regional Medical Center Indicted for Healthcare Fraud — Rare Hospital Indictment
actions.description: A federal grand jury in the Eastern District of Virginia indicted Chesapeake Regional Medical Center for conspiracy to commit healthcare fraud and obstruction in connection with enabling Dr. Javaid Perwaiz to perform thousands of medically unnecessary surgeries (hysterectomies, elective inductions) from 2010–2019. CRMC allegedly received approximately $18.5 million in reimbursements from Medicare, Medicaid, and TRICARE for these procedures. This is among the rarest instances of a hospital entity — rather than individuals — being criminally charged for healthcare fraud.

actions.title: DC Solar Attorney Sentenced to over 11 Years in Prison for His Involvement in the DC Solar Billion Dollar Ponzi Scheme
actions.description: Ari J. Lauer, 61, of Lafayette, was sentenced today to 11 years and five months years in prison for his role in the biggest criminal fraud scheme in the history of the Eastern District of California.

actions.title: Pennsylvania Brothers Convicted of Decades Long Racketeering Conspiracy
actions.description: Three individuals were convicted today at trial in connection with a racketeering conspiracy encompassing multiple fraud schemes, including visa fraud to employ foreign workers unlawfully, soliciting salary kickbacks from the employed workers, and health care fraud billing schemes resulting in over $32 million in losses to Pennsylvania Medicaid.
